channel.title: Recent on Brian Efflandt Coding Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Recent on Brian Efflandt Coding Portfolio

item.title: Resume Analizer (Full Stack, AI)
item.description: AI‑Powered Resume Analyzer built with React, React Router, Puter, Puter.js, Tailwind CSS, Typescript, Zustand, and Vite. This is a streamlined platform where users can authenticate, upload resumes, and receive AI‑driven insights (including ATS scoring), job‑match analysis, and targeted improvement suggestions. This is all delivered through a clean, reusable UI.

item.title: Trivia Game (JS, Node, Express)
item.description: Application that utilizes Open Trivia DB API, node js, and express. Weighted scores based on how fast you answer questions, enter your name after the quiz to be added to the (Pantry) database. If you place in the top 10, your score will be highlighted to represent your position among the leaders.

item.title: Full Stack Weather App (MERN)
item.description: MERN application that grabs an openweather API and stores the JSON in MongoDB. There is also a function that stores a timestamp for each submit in local storage that can be cleared from within the app.

item.title: Translation App (Django)
item.description: Django app that allows a user to enter text into a textarea which is then processed by the googletrans API, and then returns the translation from the previously selected language.

item.title: Mary Efflandt Photo (HTML)
item.description: Advanced web-dev semester long project which included wire frames, marketing concepts, color scheming, search engine optimization, software development life cycle, and time management concepts. The whole project involved sharing ideas with other classmates throughout the semester, and sharing constructive criticism about other people’s ideas and workflows. Notes: Bootstrap, JavaScript, SDLC, SEO, wire framing

item.title: ROBO FRIENDS (React)
item.description: React app that uses API’s to populate images and users alike. The search function allows narrowing down users (clear it to get back to origin). One API generates user images based on their name, the other populates the users themselves.
